reviews.review_description: Creating an rts  style game in vr is already challenging enough. To make a SPACE rts? Thats exactly what this studio has done. The game is fun with an interesting campaign and clever control dynamics. As an old school lover of Homeworld, this game accurately captures the vibe and feel of the originals. Definitely recommend.

developer_response.body: We're thrilled to hear you enjoyed Homeworld: Vast Reaches! Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts and for the kind words.

reviews.review_description:
This is very basic stripped down version of homeworld. It lacks the finer control of every aspect you'd normally get playing homeworld.  You cannot control any details with the strike groups. Can't dock them for repairs. You basically follow a lame story mode that you have to play a certain way for each mission or you won't advance. Homeworld is supposed to be all about strategy. 
The boring missions are bad enough. 
The game also constantly crashes or locks up. Making it worthless to even play.
Update:
11/26/25
Game still crashes. 
This is not what I would expect from a homeworld game. Each mission you start with a different amount of RU and different amounts and types of ships in your fleet. It doesn't matter what you had when you finished the previous mission.  Mission objectives shouldn't be so close that you can see them without needing sensors or to do some exploring first.  Leaves no room to build your own strategy. No to mention the very limited resources available for resources collectors to go after.

developer_response.body:
Hi xrmadman, we’re aware that some players are experiencing crashes during gameplay, and we understand how frustrating that can be. Our team is working closely with our engine partner to identify the cause and release a fix as soon as possible. 

Please email support@farbridge.com with a detailed report we can forward to our team.

Thank you for your patience as we work toward a permanent solution.

reviews.review_description:
After the crushing disappointment that was Homeworld 3 defecating over the series’ legacy I had low hopes for this.

But, while somewhat basic, it feels like Homeworld by people who loved and understood the originals and I’m grateful to have experienced it.

I’m among the many who pines for a skirmish mode so that it won’t have an ending.

Edit: Thanks for the new content. I appreciate any extra time I can have in this gem and hope edits help your metrics.

developer_response.body: We appreciate your review, Eunostos! Our developers are huge Homeworld fans and invested a lot of thought and intention into this game; we're excited to hear that you enjoyed the new content.

reviews.review_description:
What an immersive game, the control system is amazing and the different ways to control the attack groups can take some practise so don't ignore the tutorial! I lost so much time in this game just trying to give it a look, before I knew it, it was lunch time!

There are some comments about crashes but I have found that pressing the meta button to go to the normal quit screen, means that when you go back without quitting, the game comes back

developer_response.body: We're thrilled to hear you enjoyed Homeworld: Vast Reaches! Our latest update features new challenge levels, enhanced visuals, and performance improvements that we hope you will also appreciate.

reviews.review_description: This game brings me back so many memories like a carrier have been lost to sea of sands, to feet two memberships working together to finish up a bad. ￼ I love it when you guys put it in VR see you guys in the battlefield.!￼

developer_response.body: We're thrilled to hear you enjoyed Homeworld: Vast Reaches! Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts.

reviews.review_title: Needs a skirmish mode & mid level save
reviews.review_description:
If the developers had access to the franchise, why didn’t they use the original audio and speech files? 

The game has bugs as in a few levels ￼ the mothership disappeared, but you could still click on the outline. Also had the same issue with destroyers on the last level.

I did not have any crash issues, probably due to the new update, running on quest 3.

The control system needs tweaking.

It desperately needs a skirmish mode so you can play either side  against the AI on the 11 maps .

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We will share your notes about the bugs you encountered and your suggestions for the control systems with our team.

reviews.review_title: True to form but crashes constantly
reviews.review_description:
The game follows its roots, the adaptation to VR controls is amazing, and now it really feels you're the Mothership... 
Unfortunately, the game constantly crashes to a point where it induces nausea and even Horizon OS is completely unresponsive. Sometimes the only way to solve it is to force restart the headset. 

This would be less bad if there were checkpoints on levels as well, so you could quickly jump back in the action after a restart, but after investing 20m into a level and having to fully restart it is just demotivating 😴

There are also multiple reports here stating the same, and the fact there is no update to fix this is just annoying all together. Please get it together and issue an update.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, we’re aware that some players are experiencing crashes during gameplay, and we understand how frustrating that can be. Our team is working closely with our engine partner to identify the cause and release a fix as soon as possible. 

Today’s content update should resolve these issues. If problems continue, please email support@farbridge.com with a detailed report.

Thank you for your patience as we work toward a permanent solution.

reviews.review_title: A bit glitchy, but true to form!
reviews.review_description: ￼￼ sure the game crashes on a few missions and the dialogue script could probably have been reviewed a bit more, but overall a fantastic game that keeps the Homeworld narrative going. I’ve tried a few space RTS ￼VR games on the quest and this one is definitely in the top three.

developer_response.body: We're thrilled to hear you enjoyed Homeworld: Vast Reaches! Our latest update features new challenge levels, enhanced visuals, and performance improvements that we hope you will also appreciate.

reviews.review_description: Stuck not even halfway through the campaign.  Game freezes over and over.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, we’re aware that some players are experiencing crashes during gameplay, and we understand how frustrating that can be. Our team is working closely with our engine partner to identify the cause and release a fix as soon as possible. 

Today’s update should resolve these issues. If problems continue, please email support@farbridge.com with a detailed report.

Thank you for your patience as we work toward a permanent solution.

reviews.review_description: Disappointed. I'm not sure whether the other reviewers have ever played a Homeworld game? This feels like a very heavily cut-down, uninspired entry to the Homeworld series. Gone are the detailed strategy ideas of 1 & 2, like selectable strike group formations, aggressiveness and defensive selections and the detailed strategic controls that the earlier entries had. Map sizes feel small, yet still incredibly sparse compared to the previous entries as gone are things like the beautiful clouds of dust and massive structures, and where moving the mothership could be part of your strategy, you can't move it at all in this one as it just sits there like a big fat sitting duck. No user unlockable tech-trees to enhance particular strategies. Gone are the lovely cinematics where newly constructed ships would deploy from the mothership construction bays, here they just teleport in where-ever the strike group happens to be. Even the textures and models seem poor, even compared to the original 2000's era games! Biggest issue I have is the frequent lockups, where the headset crashes with a fixed view; sometimes it comes back from the Occulus/Meta menu, but if in the middle of battle, tough, you just lost everything as they sit there. Maybe recommend as a cheap pickup if they fix the lockups.

Had a wave of nostalgia when I saw this app available in the store.
Graphics look great and true to the original, but that’s where the similarity ends.
Game constantly freezes and crashes and I lose all current content.  I read that bug fixes have been sent out, but not sure if it has got to mine yet.  Would really like to see this fixed, or it will be a quick and disappointing end to a trip down memory !

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review. We hope that today’s update resolves the crashes you’ve been experiencing. We are aware that some players encounter crashes during gameplay, and we understand how frustrating this can be. Our team is working closely with our engine partner to identify the cause and release a fix as soon as possible.

Thank you for your patience as we work toward a permanent solution.

reviews.review_description:
Amazing graphic effects, accurate model, and original vibe of Homeworld.
A bit tricky to get hands on in the beginning like all HomeWorld games, but having a floating mothership in your living room? that's mind blowing experience!! great great game!

Just wanted to say thanks to the developer and publisher of this game. I highly recommend it to all Homeworld fans.

Homeworld was one of the first RTS games I ever played, years ago and Homeworld games, including Deserts of Kharaak are one of my favourite games ever.

To play this in VR is dream come true to me. 

The visuals, audio, and the story are all authentic and so much fun. I would love to see a skirmish mode and DLC!!! I literally have to stop myself from playing the game just to save it for later..

Thank you!

On quest 3. Screen just locks up then goes again after 10 to 15 seconds during some of the campaigns. Very annoying. No way to adjust resolution or refresh rate.  Devs should have fixed this before putting it on sale. Otherwise game is pretty good.

developer_response.body: We haven't heard of that particular problem before. Can you email more details to support-hwvr@farbridge.com ? It would be good to know which missions and difficulty levels this happens on.

reviews.review_title: Love the game so far, and devs listen
reviews.review_description:
I'm on mission 5, hard difficulty but the mission stops after starting. Too many ships lost, retreat, retreat. My campaign seems bugged out. Can't continue. Love the game is far, controls are great. I love zooming in and following the ships. Waypoint system is really helpful. Please help devs to continue my campaign. 

Update, it's fixed!

The Homeworld IP carries this game. Lots of missing command and control elements that are present in strategy games like this but are missing in this one.

Complete inability to create additional control groups is a baffling decision especially when ships cannot exist outside control groups.

Missions last about 5 minutes and are very simple.

I'll keep it because it's Homeworld but it's definitely not good Homeworld.

reviews.review_title: Some fun here but overall Underwhelmed
reviews.review_description:
So I was really excited to see a franchise RTS to come to Quest. The execution was, as the title suggests, underwhelming. The story is told in audio narration over static, vague, graphic comic panels. The 3D enviornments in the play space are again, very static, flat looking backgrounds against which the 3d action takes place.
Ok so I can roll with that. Then the game play...so I like the wrist interface and control over the units. Units are controlled in groups but they are very small and non descrip. You can expand the size of things but the models don't look that great expanded and the play space gets difficult to manage when streched. And here's where I struggle, units are grouped and it's not clear how to best group the units to attack or defend against attack. And when you do attack there isn't a sense of strategy around the battle. Just throw a group at the group coming at you then build back up.
So reading over this I see it sounds really negative. Some of this is related to having high expectations for the title. There is some fun here and interesting challenges just set your expectations accordingly.

I played all HW franchise games. This one is almost perfect. Why one star? It crashes constantly. I'm lucky if I can complete a mission without crashing. 

With that pricetag, a game should be thoroughly tested before offering it to players.

I will instantly change to five stars once that issue is fixed.

developer_response.body: Sorry that you ran into these problems. We just released an update to the game that fixes some crashes and bugs. We'd love it if you gave the game another try.

reviews.review_description:
I own all the homeworld games from the first one in 1999 to the Homeworld 3 released this year. Vast reaches was the last I bought and since I started playing VR titles, I dreamed about playing Homeworld in a VR platfform. 
Vast reaches could be the game I expected, mainly for its polished interface (despite being a simple one), good graphics considering the quest limitations and a story more like the older titles than the terrible one from the homeworld 3. But unfortunetaly it lacks one of the main features of a homeworld game which is Skirmish mode. I would like to play a multiplayer game also, but a Skirmish mode is really necessary.
Another good addition would be a roguelike mode like what is available in homeworld 3.
I only gave 3 stars for this game because, in my opinion, right now  it lacks content for a US$ 29 price tag.
The controls in this game are very awkward, and the tutorial won't allow me to continue. The game is literally unplayable. Total waste of time and money.
Even recent updates did not helped. Average 3 crashes to complete each mission.

I have fond memories of this game from many years ago on my PC 
I was in a very stressful job. I found that a great way to relax was play this game, the music was the first thing that relaxed me, the graphics and colours made it so appealing. The game play I picked up. And now I am delighted to play this again in amazing VR what a treat.

Thankful to Farbridge & Gearbox for this even existing as well as the upcoming HW3 release. Early gameplay impressions are overall positive. The controls are really good. Expectations should be set at HW1 levels not HW remastered. I’d like to see a quest 3 update that uses the remastered assets for ships, skyboxes, weapon effects, hulls, explosions, environments. Unless I’m missing something skirmish isn’t available right now or more factions. 

Some issues I noticed, the fighters are hyper spacing in? Why aren’t they being manufactured and exiting the mothership hanger? When the mothership hyper-spaced to the next level the fighters weren’t brought in to dock. They showed up on the next level, but after  S’Jet to treat the them carefully just hyper spacing away seemed weird.

Hope this sells well across platforms  and maybe we can get a VR version of the trilogy, cataclysm, mobile game, and Deserts of kharak

reviews.review_title: Homeworld on Quest, I’d like to see more
reviews.review_description: I’m giving this 5 Stars, but 4 is probably more fair. The game is Homeworld, so I am feeling generous. The game is fun, but quite a bit more simplified from the flat original. This may have been a good choice in order to keep the game accessible to new players. I’d like to see an ‘advanced’ version in the future that adds back a few details like docking and ships being created in the mothership instead of teleporting to the group. Also the combat should be a bit more slow, so we can appreciate that the fight and have a bit of time to react.

reviews.review_title: How RTS should be done in VR
reviews.review_description:
Enjoyed the original homeworld games, had mixed feelings buying this as often VR versions of games are very barebones, but on this occasion I’m very happy to be wrong. This exceeded my expectations, the plot is decent, the controls though complicated, are intuitive and they quickly felt natural. 
I’m only a few hours in but The missions and pacing are well balanced. In short it’s everything I expected from a homeworld game. 
It does feel a little strange seeing new ships hyperspace in rather than be built on the mothership but I can live with that.
Only 2 concerns I do have is :-
A: I can’t seem to move the mothership, I dislike not being able to choose my battlefield. 
B:I feel the devs need to add a skirmish mode to keep replay value after the main quest. 
I look forward to see where this game goes on the future.

At last Homeworld in VR!!! Not only that you can optionally play it mixed reality mode and see the space ships flying around your living room..

lin(k)tr(DOT)ee/accreed

Please mark review as helpful
Great time, makes me miss the first homeworld,  any fan of the series will love the mothership floating next to you like a giant tower in vr .  Developers please give us a skirmish mode!

Im not impressed. I been trying to build the scout in the tutorial portion and cannot do it. I can mine the minerals but when ir comes to getting the resource ship to join with squadron A, nothing happen. And since the mother sip is the one that builds the ships, i cant even get it to dock. It is a solid pain in the butt. Due to the difficulty to do any building in the game i have to give it a 2 star. It needs a lot more work to be user friendly.

reviews.review_title: YES IM LOVING PLAYING HOMEWORLD IN VR
reviews.review_description:
If you like eternal starlight then like me you will love this .
25 % off this and loads more       25 % off    bio . link / jekil    (remove spaces)

needed a cool RTS. and PD said its good so 5 stars just for bringing it to the quest.
Now if Rockstarr would stop BSn.
......can we get a quest 3 overhaul tho?.
Updated ships and more factions to play in skirmish mode please. It can run it with ease i promise. Thanks in advance

reviews.review_title: great game with two big things missing
reviews.review_description:
I first played a Homeworld demo which came with a PC magazine in around 2001 and fell in love with this franchise. The magazine also featured some great VR game concepts showcasing a RTS game played with only your hands and it stuck with me from when I was as a child and always wanted to experience this high tech future… and here it is and is better than imagined.

My only wish is for you guys to implement skirmish with bots and maybe multiplayer. Good luck and thanks for bringing this game in VR

reviews.review_title: Homeworld was brilliant.  Not so here
reviews.review_description:
Many years ago, I picked up a brand new game called Homeworld and it was one of those groundbreaking moments.   It was well thought through as a story, had an emersive feel, felt polished and complete, and just kept you playing for a long time... It was simply one of the best games of a generation.

This, not so much.   It's story feels unexciting, it is very buggy and very very short.  This software was not ready for release and it shows.

Put it this way, I tried to get my money back but I must have played it more than the two hours.   

Do yourself a favour and buy Eternal Starlight instead.  It's a way better game than this and something that has loads of playability after completing the campaign.

developer_response.body: Sorry that you ran into problems playing the game. We just released an update that should fix the crashes and bugs discovered post-launch. We'd love it if you gave the game another try.

reviews.review_description:
I love rts games and this on vr was amazing. 

Anyone who gets this game will not be disappointed to the point I want MORE. 

Yes developers, please make another or lots of add ons for this one please. 

Easy to learn, no complicated controls and lots of fun. 

Hope you all enjoy as much as I have and still am as have to do all difficulty modes. 

An absolute pleasure, thank you devs, I am at your mercy for more of this. 

Have a great time people.
It's what you expect.   Not far into it. But it feel like home world.   Good hold over while I wait for hw3

Went back to play after the speedy update and played for a couple of hours! It’s great to be able to go back to a new familiar game from my past. Thanks for the bug fixes. 

I recommend.

developer_response.body: We've recently released an update to the game, and it sounds like these problems have been fixed. We'd love it if you gave the game another try.

reviews.review_description: Game looks fantastic but falls short on gameplay. Missions are too short and scripted. It was enjoyable but I finished it in about 5 hours of play.

reviews.review_title: I loved this title many years ago
reviews.review_description: And VR version builds on that, it’s truly an amazing game. I hope additional content comes out, I’d love a port of the original story using this engine.
They did such a good job on adapting the gameplay to vr. When the original homeworld came out I used to dream about a VR version of the game, and this is the dream brought to life.

The good reviews are from people that never played Homeworld before, or totally forgot how it used to be.

The good:
- True to the original Homeworld design  and atmosphere.

The bad:
#1. No sense of scale. Ships look tiny, and zoom in/scale in the focus distance ruins the sense of scale. Ships don't have enough detail.

#2. There is no real battle. Ships explode within seconds. You kill an entiry enemy fleet within 10 seconds. No time to act or even admire the battle.

#3. Ultra simplified. Yes, some simplifications are good ideas... But mothership that doesn't even move? No firing, no docking, no subsystems... etc.

I had high hopes for this. It only made me nostalgic to go play the old games.
The storyline and the gameplay are enjoyable so far.
